title: [Feature]: Android support
body:
### Feature request description

I want to use podman in an Android environment. But I can't find the tutorial. It's great if podman is friendly enough to support use in an Android environment.

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @octavei.  Podman is technically capable of running on Android but I do not know of an effort in that space yet.

Cc: @containers/podman-maintainers
I thought I remembered someone from community playing with it, but I can not find it now.

> I thought I remembered someone from community playing with it, but I can not find it now.

If you can help find this project or tutorial, I would really appreciate it. I now need to run containers in my phone, but I don't really know much about podman's technical implementation.

The major roadblock is that typically phone kernels don't have proper support for cgroups, namespaces, overlayfs etc. So a [patched custom kernel](https://gist.github.com/FreddieOliveira/efe850df7ff3951cb62d74bd770dce27#22-kernel) would be required to run podman natively, severely limiting availability.

IMO a more viable approach would be to use a minimal vm/kernel with unprivileged qemu for legacy devices (probably too slow) and rootless [pKVM](https://source.android.com/docs/core/virtualization/architecture) for android 13+ devices like the Pixel 7. ([related but outdated info](https://android.googlesource.com/platform/packages/modules/Virtualization/+/HEAD/docs/getting_started/index.md#running-demo-app))
The closest implementation of this approach, I have seen, is @kdrag0n's Nestbox (closed source & not using podman).
Though that's probably beyond the scope of this project.

You could tinker with qemu in termux for now.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think this is more of a discussion than an issue.
